subject: English
chapter: Journey to the End of the Earth

cases.passage: Mark Twain's travelogue, 'Journey to the End of the Earth', highlights the importance of exploration and travel. While reading the travelogue, Rohan, a Class 12 student, felt inspired to embark on a journey to the Himalayas. He wanted to experience the thrill of trekking and witness the breathtaking beauty of the mountains. Rohan's parents, though initially hesitant, eventually agreed to let him go on the condition that he would be accompanied by an experienced guide. As Rohan ascended higher, he encountered diverse landscapes, cultures, and people, which broadened his perspective and understanding of the world.

questions.q: What inspired Rohan to embark on a journey to the Himalayas?
opts: (A) A school trip
(B) Mark Twain's travelogue
(C) A friend's recommendation
(D) A family tradition
questions.explanation: The passage states that Rohan felt inspired to embark on a journey after reading Mark Twain's travelogue.

questions.q: Why were Rohan's parents initially hesitant to let him go on the journey?
opts: (A) They were concerned about the cost
(B) They were worried about Rohan's safety
(C) They did not want Rohan to miss school
(D) They did not like the idea of trekking
questions.explanation: Although the passage does not explicitly state the reason, it can be inferred that Rohan's parents were worried about his safety, as they agreed to let him go only if he was accompanied by an experienced guide.

questions.q: What did Rohan encounter during his journey to the Himalayas?
opts: (A) Only diverse landscapes
(B) Only different cultures
(C) Only various people
(D) Diverse landscapes, cultures, and people
questions.explanation: The passage states that Rohan encountered diverse landscapes, cultures, and people during his journey.

questions.q: What was the condition set by Rohan's parents for him to go on the journey?
opts: (A) That he would return within a week
(B) That he would be accompanied by an experienced guide
(C) That he would not trek alone
(D) That he would not go to high altitudes
questions.explanation: The passage states that Rohan's parents agreed to let him go on the condition that he would be accompanied by an experienced guide.

questions.q: What was the outcome of Rohan's journey to the Himalayas?
opts: (A) He got lost in the mountains
(B) He suffered from altitude sickness
(C) His perspective and understanding of the world broadened
(D) He decided to become a professional trekker
questions.explanation: The passage states that Rohan's perspective and understanding of the world broadened as a result of his journey.

cases.passage: As Rohan reflected on his journey to the Himalayas, he realized that it was not just a physical journey, but also an emotional and intellectual one. He had learned to appreciate the beauty of nature, the importance of human relationships, and the value of perseverance. Rohan's experience had taught him that travel and exploration can be transformative, allowing individuals to discover new aspects of themselves and the world around them. He decided to share his story with others, hoping to inspire them to embark on their own journeys of self-discovery. By writing about his experiences, Rohan aimed to convey the significance of travel in broadening one's perspective and fostering personal growth.

questions.q: What did Rohan realize about his journey to the Himalayas?
opts: (A) That it was a waste of time
(B) That it was only a physical journey
(C) That it was not worth the effort
(D) That it was not just a physical journey, but also an emotional and intellectual one
questions.explanation: The passage states that Rohan realized his journey was not just a physical journey, but also an emotional and intellectual one.

questions.q: What did Rohan learn to appreciate during his journey?
opts: (A) Only the beauty of nature
(B) Only the importance of human relationships
(C) Only the value of perseverance
(D) The beauty of nature, the importance of human relationships, and the value of perseverance
questions.explanation: The passage states that Rohan learned to appreciate the beauty of nature, the importance of human relationships, and the value of perseverance.

questions.q: What did Rohan decide to do after reflecting on his journey?
opts: (A) To keep his experience to himself
(B) To share his story with others
(C) To write a book about his journey
(D) To become a travel guide
questions.explanation: The passage states that Rohan decided to share his story with others, hoping to inspire them to embark on their own journeys of self-discovery.

questions.q: What was Rohan's aim in writing about his experiences?
opts: (A) To entertain his readers
(B) To convey the significance of travel in broadening one's perspective and fostering personal growth
(C) To persuade others to trek in the Himalayas
(D) To describe the landscapes he encountered
questions.explanation: The passage states that Rohan aimed to convey the significance of travel in broadening one's perspective and fostering personal growth.

questions.q: What does Rohan's story suggest about the impact of travel on individuals?
opts: (A) That it has no significant impact
(B) That it can be transformative, allowing individuals to discover new aspects of themselves and the world around them
(C) That it is only physically challenging
(D) That it is a waste of time and money
questions.explanation: The passage suggests that travel can be transformative, allowing individuals to discover new aspects of themselves and the world around them.
